source_jobs.title: Finance Intern (SG)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT K-ID

k-ID is the global leader in privacy-first compliance and age verification infrastructure. Recognized as one of TIME’s Best Inventions of 2025, named a Tech Pioneer by the World Economic Forum and a winner of Fast Company’s Next Big Things in Tech, we are building the Age Layer for the internet—the fundamental infrastructure that allows digital platforms to verify age and manage compliance globally without friction.

Our core platform, anchored by the Compliance Development Kit (CDK) and AgeKit, is the trusted engine for the world’s largest game publishers and digital ecosystems. We replace fragmented, manual compliance with a unified API that handles age verification, parental consent, and regulatory logic across 200+ markets. Backed by top-tier venture capital firms like a16z and Lightspeed, k-ID is entering a phase of growth to define the standard for global digital safety.





ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Finance Intern to support our finance operations and reporting processes. This role offers a unique opportunity to contribute to the modernization of our financial systems, including the adoption of AI and innovative technologies as part of our transformation roadmap starting in Q2 2026.

As an intern, you will gain hands-on experience across key finance functions such as financial reporting, audit support, expense management, and process improvement. You will also play a meaningful role in enhancing efficiency by supporting the development and implementation of automation tools and data-driven solutions.

Working closely with the Finance team and cross-functional stakeholders, you will help ensure the accuracy, integrity, and compliance of financial operations. This position is ideal for candidates with an accounting background who are analytical, tech-savvy, and eager to apply their skills in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop, implement, and scale AI-driven solutions to enhance budget planning and review processes

 - Design and build automation tools for revenue tracking, purchasing workflows, and financial reporting

 - Research AI models and algorithms to identify practical applications for financial and business challenges

 - Collaborate with finance and engineering teams to test, validate, and deploy automation initiatives

 - Support end-to-end finance operations, including accounts payable, claims processing, and reconciliations

 - Assist in the preparation of monthly financial reports and variance analysis

 - Participate in audit activities by preparing supporting schedules and documentation

 - Work with internal stakeholders to gather financial data and resolve discrepancies

 - Support ad-hoc tasks and projects assigned by the Finance Manager




STRETCH / VALUE-ADD PROJECTS:

 - Develop automated dashboards or reporting templates

 - Support finance workflows and propose improvements.

 - Support simple data analytics or visualization tasks (skilled in Power BI, Python)




REQUIREMENTS

 - Currently pursuing a degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field

 - Strong understanding of basic accounting principles.

 - High attention to detail, strong analytical and problem solving skills.

 - Good communication and interpersonal skills

 - A minimum commitment of 16 weeks.

Applicants Privacy Policy https://k-id.com/job-applicants-privacy-notice

source_jobs.title: Product Internship Program 2026
source_jobs.description:
k-ID is a trailblazing tech startup, specializing in providing advanced, privacy- centric tools designed to protect and empower kids and teens online. We have secured pivotal partnerships with key industry leaders in the gaming industry and are venture-backed by leading global VCs. We are poised for unprecedented growth and expansion in the coming year.

At k-ID, we're on a mission to revolutionize digital safety and privacy for kids and teens. Our internship program in Singapore offers talented students the opportunity to work on cutting-edge projects that directly impact young users' online experiences.

Join our dynamic team in Singapore and contribute to meaningful projects while gaining invaluable industry experience. We're looking for passionate students from universities, polytechnics, and specialized programs who are ready to innovate in the digital safety space.




PROGRAM OVERVIEW

We are looking for a top‑tier Human–Computer Interaction and developer‑experience intern to help shape K‑ID’s Compliance Studio into the gold‑standard developer portal. The ideal candidates are hands‑on HCI/UX practitioners who love turning complex systems into crisp, testable workflows. You’ll work across research, design, and prototyping to make compliance orchestration feel like product velocity for developers.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

UNDERTSTAND

 - Partner with engineers and product to map the top developer jobs: stand‑up environment, configure multi‑provider waterfalls, run market‑specific policy variants, and generate audit‑ready reports

 - Run a rapid heuristic review and IA audit against HCI best practices

DISCOVER

 - Lead 6–10 usability sessions with target developer profiles

 - Instrument a baseline for time‑to‑first‑success, task success rate, and error types

DESIGN

 - Redesign navigation and interaction patterns with progressive disclosure and smart defaults

 - Write high‑clarity microcopy and inline guidance to reduce doc lookups

PROTOTYPE

 - Build high‑fidelity, clickable flows for 2–3 critical journeys and connect them to sample data

VALIDATE

 - Compare baseline vs. prototype with usability tests and accessibility checks

 - Package a phased rollout with engineering feasibility notes





REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related technical field with strong academic standing

 - Proficiency in programming languages (Python, TypeScript, JavaScript, etc) with demonstrated experience through coursework, projects, or previous internships

 - Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with ability to learn new technologies quickly and work collaboratively in a team environment

 - Experience with fundamental development tools including databases, and web technologies

 - Self-motivated with excellent communication skills and genuine interest in enterprise software development




PROGRAM BENEFITS

 - Mentorship from Global Experts: Learn directly from k-ID's global Product, Design & Engineering team

 - Cross-Functional Exposure: Interact with product, design, GTM and compliance teams

 - Commercial Skills: Learn to breakdown and communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical professionals

 - Tech Industry Insight: Gain firsthand experience in the growing digital safety and privacy sector




PROGRAM DETAILS

 - Duration: Min. 12 weeks

 - Location: Hybrid working at our Singapore office



Applicants Privacy Policy https://k-id.com/job-applicants-privacy-notice

source_jobs.title: Legal Internship Program 2026
source_jobs.description:
k-ID is a trailblazing tech startup, specializing in providing advanced, privacy- centric tools designed to protect and empower kids and teens online. We have secured pivotal partnerships with key industry leaders in the gaming industry and are venture-backed by leading global VCs. We are poised for unprecedented growth and expansion in the coming year.

At k-ID, we're on a mission to revolutionize digital safety and privacy for kids and teens. Our internship program in Singapore offers talented students the opportunity to work on cutting-edge projects that directly impact young users' online experiences.

Join our dynamic team in Singapore and contribute to meaningful projects while gaining invaluable industry experience. We're looking for passionate students from universities, polytechnics, and specialized programs who are ready to innovate in the digital safety space.




OVERVIEW

This internship aims to introduce the interns to the realm of online services and age-related legal, regulatory and compliance issues these services face. These range from games to messaging apps to social media - interns will be introduced to the key issues these businesses face in relation to kids and teens and how these issues are tackled on a global basis. Interns will find themselves having to apply their legal research skills in a manner completely different from their academic journey thus far and will gain insight on how the tech industry operates on a regional and global basis.




WHAT YOU'LL DO




REGULATORY COMPLIANCE & RESEARCH

 - Research and review of existing age-related and privacy-related laws across 190+ countries (with guidance and feedback), with a focus on presenting your research findings in a pragmatic and concise manner, as well as keeping up with new laws / regulations and how they impact online service providers and their users


COMMERCIAL LEGAL

 - Drafting, reviewing and understanding how standard corporate and commercial legal documents work and applying your legal knowledge in the corporate/commercial setting

 - To assist Legal Counsels with doing compliance assessment reports for clients/online service providers




REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently enrolled in a LLB (Bachelor of Laws) or LLM (Master of Laws) at a Singapore University

 - Strong research and analytical skills

 - Interest in technology, privacy law, and digital safety

 - Excellent written and verbal communication

 - Commitment to full-time participation for the program duration




PROGRAM BENEFITS

 - Mentorship from Global Experts: Learn directly from k-ID's Chief Legal Officer and legal team

 - Practical Legal Experience: Apply academic knowledge in a commercial tech environment

 - Cross-Functional Exposure: Interact with product, engineering, and compliance teams

 - Legal Research Skill Development: Enhance research capabilities with real-world applications

 - Commercial Translation Skills: Learn to communicate complex legal concepts to non-legal professionals

 - Tech Industry Insight: Gain firsthand experience in the growing digital safety and privacy sector




PROGRAM DETAILS

 - Duration: Min. 12 weeks

 - Location: Hybrid working at our Singapore office





HOW TO APPLY

This is a specialized opportunity for students in the Law program. Interested candidates should:

 - Submit your CV and a brief statement of interest

 - Selected students will be going through a short interview with the Legal team



Join us in creating a safer digital world for young people through innovative legal approaches!



Applicants Privacy Policy https://k-id.com/job-applicants-privacy-notice

source_jobs.title: Engineering Internship Program 2026
source_jobs.description:
k-ID is a trailblazing tech startup, specializing in providing advanced, privacy- centric tools designed to protect and empower kids and teens online. We have secured pivotal partnerships with key industry leaders in the gaming industry and are venture-backed by leading global VCs. We are poised for unprecedented growth and expansion in the coming year.

At k-ID, we're on a mission to revolutionize digital safety and privacy for kids and teens. Our internship program in Singapore offers talented students the opportunity to work on cutting-edge projects that directly impact young users' online experiences.

Join our dynamic team in Singapore and contribute to meaningful projects while gaining invaluable industry experience. We're looking for passionate students from universities, polytechnics, and specialized programs who are ready to innovate in the digital safety space.




PROGRAM OVERVIEW

You will be working alongside the Singapore engineers on the Product, Design & Engineering team to deliver features and fix bugs in the Compliance Studio and Family Connect products. You will work across a range of technologies such as TypeScript (Next.js), GOlang and user flows.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Start with smaller, well-scoped development tasks to learn the k-ID platform codebase and development workflows

 - Progress to increasingly complex projects as you build technical skills and understanding of the platform architecture

 - Work toward delivering a major feature, with the primary goal of developing the Compliance Studio functionality

 - Contribute to high-impact platform improvements that deliver significant value to end users

 - Collaborate with senior developers and product teams to understand requirements and best practices

 - Gain hands-on experience building enterprise-grade features from concept through implementation





REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related technical field with strong academic standing

 - Proficiency in one of our primary languages (Go, React, TypeScript) with demonstrated experience through previous internships

 - Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with ability to learn new technologies quickly and work collaboratively in a team environment

 - Experience with fundamental development tools including databases, and web technologies

 - Self-motivated with excellent communication skills and genuine interest in enterprise software development




PROGRAM BENEFITS

 - Mentorship from Global Experts: Learn directly from k-ID's global Engineering team

 - Cross-Functional Exposure: Interact with product, design, and compliance teams

 - Commercial Skills: Learn to breakdown and communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical professionals

 - Tech Industry Insight: Gain firsthand experience in the growing digital safety and privacy sector




PROGRAM DETAILS

 - Duration: Min. 16 weeks

 - Location: Hybrid working at our Singapore office



Applicants Privacy Policy https://k-id.com/job-applicants-privacy-notice

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer - SG
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT K-ID

k-ID is the global leader in privacy-first compliance and age verification infrastructure. Recognized as one of TIME’s Best Inventions of 2025, named a Tech Pioneer by the World Economic Forum and a winner of Fast Company’s Next Big Things in Tech, we are building the Age Layer for the internet—the fundamental infrastructure that allows digital platforms to verify age and manage compliance globally without friction.

Our core platform, anchored by the Compliance Development Kit (CDK) and AgeKit, is the trusted engine for the world’s largest game publishers and digital ecosystems. We replace fragmented, manual compliance with a unified API that handles age verification, parental consent, and regulatory logic across 200+ markets. Backed by top-tier venture capital firms like a16z and Lightspeed, k-ID is entering a phase of growth to define the standard for global digital safety.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Full Stack Engineer at k-ID, you will help build the web applications and product experiences that developers, families, and end users rely on to interact with our platform.

This is a hands-on engineering role for someone who is comfortable working across the stack and wants real ownership. You will work closely with product, design, legal, and engineering partners to ship high quality applications that are fast, reliable, and easy to use. You should be comfortable moving between frontend and backend work, contributing to technical design, and operating what you build in production.

We are looking for an engineer who can execute independently, make sound technical decisions, and thrive in an early stage environment where systems and processes are still taking shape.




LOCATION & LANGUAGE

 - Location: Singapore

 - Languages: Proficiency in English
   
   


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Build and maintain full stack web applications that support k-ID’s platform, partners, and end users

 - Contribute to technical design and implementation decisions across frontend, backend, APIs, and data flows

 - Work with product managers, designers, and legal stakeholders to turn complex requirements into clear and usable product experiences

 - Develop responsive, reliable, and maintainable applications using Next.js and related technologies

 - Improve and support production systems by identifying issues, debugging effectively, and shipping fixes with care

 - Migrate existing systems and workflows to newer architectures and data stores while preserving system integrity and business continuity

 - Write clean, well tested code and help maintain a high bar for code quality, performance, and developer experience

 - Participate in code reviews, technical discussions, and team problem solving

 - Contribute positively to the engineering culture through ownership, collaboration, and a willingness to improve how the team workse
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience

 - 2 or more years of professional software engineering experience, ideally in a startup or high growth environment

 - Strong experience with Go, TypeScript, React, and Next.js

 - Experience building modern full stack applications using technologies such as PostgreSQL, Redis, S3, and Kubernetes

 - Experience with server rendered web applications and modern frontend architecture

 - Ability to safely migrate legacy systems or workflows to modern application and data infrastructure without downtime or data loss

 - Experience partnering with designers to build polished and intuitive user experiences

 - Comfort working across product requirements, technical design, implementation, testing, and production support

 - Ability to operate effectively in a fast paced environment with ambiguity and changing priorities

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills in English

 - Familiarity with compliance related or regulated product environments is a plus, but not required
   
   


BENEFITS


COMPETITIVE SA

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager, Greater China
source_jobs.description:
CUSTOMER SUCCESS MANAGER, GREATER CHINA




ABOUT K-ID

k-ID is the global leader in privacy first compliance and age verification infrastructure. Recognized as one of TIME’s Best Inventions of 2025, named a Tech Pioneer by the World Economic Forum, and a winner of Fast Company’s Next Big Things in Tech, we are building the Age Layer for the internet, the foundational infrastructure that allows digital platforms to verify age and manage compliance globally without friction.

Our core platform, anchored by the Compliance Development Kit, or CDK, and AgeKit, is the trusted engine for some of the world’s largest game publishers and digital ecosystems. We replace fragmented, manual compliance with a unified API that handles age verification, parental consent, and regulatory logic across 200 plus markets. Backed by top tier venture capital firms like a16z and Lightspeed, k-ID is entering a phase of growth to define the standard for global digital safety.




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a commercially strong Customer Success Manager to own and grow a portfolio of strategic customers across Greater China.

This is a post sale role with responsibility for renewals, upsell, executive relationship management, and customer adoption. You will work with game publishers, digital platforms, and technology companies navigating complex privacy, safety, and age assurance requirements across multiple markets.

You will serve as the primary point of contact for assigned accounts and work closely with Product, Integration Engineering, Legal, and Customer Success to help customers launch successfully, deepen adoption, and expand their use of k-ID over time. This role requires someone who can manage complex multi stakeholder relationships across Chinese and international teams, navigate a fast moving regulatory environment, and identify commercial opportunities while strengthening long term customer trust.

This role can be based in Singapore, Hong Kong, or Mainland China.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own a portfolio of strategic Greater China accounts across renewals, upsell, and overall commercial health.

 - Build and maintain account plans aligned to customer priorities, product adoption, and regional compliance needs.

 - Develop strong relationships with stakeholders across product, legal, compliance, trust and safety, operations, and technical teams.

 - Partner closely with Integration Engineering and Legal to support onboarding, deployment, and adoption.

 - Maintain customer momentum from contract signature through launch and into expansion.

 - Lead renewal and upsell conversations, including commercial alignment, pricing discussions, and negotiation.

 - Identify opportunities to expand product usage across additional titles, business units, teams, and markets.

 - Monitor account health, surface risks early, and coordinate internal teams to resolve issues quickly.

 - Run regular business reviews and strategic check ins that keep customers aligned and moving forward.

 - Bring customer feedback and market insight back to Product, Engineering, Legal, and Go To Market teams.

 - Maintain strong CRM hygiene, account visibility, and forecast discipline in HubSpot.
   
   


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - You have 5 plus years of experience in account management, customer success, or post sale commercial roles in B2B SaaS.

 - You have a strong track record of managing strategic or enterprise accounts and owning renewals and customer growth.

 - You are comfortable operating in complex, multi stakeholder environments and building trust with both executive and working level counterparts.

 - You have strong commercial judgment and know how to balance customer partnership with revenue ownership.

 - You are organized, proactive, and able to manage multiple priorities across internal and external stakeholders.

 - You are fluent in English and Mandarin.
   
   


PREFERRED EXPERIENCE

 - Experience supporting customers in Greater China, especial

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager - Compliance Development Kit (CDK)
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW:



k-ID is building the age aware internet, where kids and teens can access technology in ways that are safer, more private, and appropriate for their age, while platforms and games can meet complex age related requirements across global markets.



Our platform helps developers understand user age, configure age appropriate experiences, manage parental consent, and stay aligned with fast changing global laws and regulations. We are building compliance infrastructure for the internet: developer first, privacy preserving, and designed to make complex regulatory obligations easier to operationalize at scale.




JOB SUMMARY:



CDK is k-ID's compliance engine. It turns regulation into developer primitives – age assurance, parental consent, permissions, jurisdictional rule resolution, entitlements – that any platform can configure in Compliance Studio, power with our regulatory intelligence engine (neimo), and enforce at runtime through clean APIs and drop-in widgets.

Today CDK is pointed at the fastest-moving regulatory category on earth: child safety and online safety law. But the engine is regulation-agnostic by design. The same machinery that resolves what a 13-year-old can do in Brazil resolves an age-restricted purchase, an AI-companion safeguard, or a rule that doesn't exist yet. That's the premise – and it's why platforms across gaming, social, AI, and commerce build on CDK, and why compliance is becoming infrastructure the way payments became an API.

We're hiring a Product Lead – a principal-level role – to own CDK end-to-end and run it like its own business. There is no playbook for this product; you will write it. You treat CDK as a platform, not a feature: you see the pattern across enterprise requests and build the abstraction, not the one-off, and you make global regulatory complexity disappear for the developer integrating you in an afternoon.

This is not a backlog-grooming role. You'll be simultaneously in the weeds on product behavior intricacies and driving overall business strategy and direction.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:



You run CDK like its own business. You're measured on outcomes, not output – adoption, conversion, expansion, and reliability, not specs written or features shipped – and you're the single point of accountability for those numbers across Legal, Engineering, Design, GTM, and Customer Success.

 - Grow CDK as a product line. Own its arc from configuration tool to the compliance platform others build on, and the adoption, revenue, and expansion that prove it. What you build, defer, and kill is judged against that outcome, not a feature backlog.

 - Land new verticals. Turn the regulation-agnostic engine into shipped revenue in new categories – AI, regulated commerce, whatever's next – by building primitives that generalize instead of one-offs per customer.

 - Define the primitive. Identify the core abstractions that make CDK genuinely differentiated (permission strings, jurisdictional rule engines, consent orchestration) and partner with engineering to build them.

 - Run the pod, not just the roadmap. You own the "what" and the "why," then you own the build – the cadence, the unblocking, the in-the-weeds calls with engineering, not a deck you hand off. Pure strategists who delegate execution won't last here; neither will pure executors who don't want to own the bet.

 - Keep it unbreakable at scale. CDK enforces compliance for tens of millions of users; staying correct as the law shifts underneath is an outcome you own with engineering, not a problem you hand to them.
   
   


MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:



 - 8+ years of product management experience, with a track record of owning technically complex platforms, infrastructure, or developer tools.

 - Exceptional product and operational judgment in high-ambiguity environments.

 - The ability to read API documentation, engage on system architecture tradeoffs, and think in systems.

 - A productization instinct: you understa

source_jobs.title: QA Analyst
source_jobs.description:
QA ANALYST



ABOUT K-ID

k-ID is building the age aware internet, where kids and teens can access technology in ways that are safer, more private, and appropriate for their age, while platforms and games can meet complex age related requirements across global markets.

Our platform helps developers understand user age, configure age appropriate experiences, manage parental consent, and stay aligned with fast changing global laws and regulations. We are building compliance infrastructure for the internet: developer first, privacy preserving, and designed to make complex regulatory obligations easier to operationalize at scale.

 


ROLE OVERVIEW

The Market Insights Operations Contractor is responsible for running k-ID’s compliance-benchmarking engine. This function analyzes how real-world games and apps handle children’s data privacy and online safety, powering our compliance benchmarks and market intelligence for customers. This is a hands-on role focused on accurate, explainable, and timely results throughout each testing cycle.

You will operate neimo’s game-testing and Market Insights pipeline — utilizing both AI agentic and manual testing — to ensure that results are reliable, explainable, and always delivered on schedule. Success in this role means making operations smoother, catching data issues early, and ensuring status visibility and resolution of blockers without escalation or chase.

 


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Plan, manage, and execute testing cycles: Define games, jurisdictions, questions, and schedules; ensure rationale is documented and visible.

 - Operate and refine third-party AI agentic testing platforms: Configure runs, refine agent instructions, review and classify outputs, and follow through with vendors.

 - Conduct detailed Data QA: Review AI outputs, check and reconcile data, diagnose trends, cross-reference with client implementations, and resolve anomalies before escalation.

 - Perform manual testing for scenarios not yet covered by agentic automation: Scope, run, and document manual tests according to repeatable SOPs.

 - Maintain clear, explainable methodology, SOPs, and result documentation for stakeholders.

 - Provide daily status and progress visibility during active cycles; ensure no backlog of QA or unresolved issues.

 - Drive blockers to resolution, clearly logging reasons and responsible owners.

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Testing plans are finalized before each cycle, with clear scope, rationale, and schedules.

 - Real-time cycle status is maintained and visible daily during testing.

 - No QA backlog: Completed runs are reviewed within two business days.

 - Data issues are detected and resolved upstream, with explanations for all trends and anomalies.

 - Every blocked or inconclusive outcome is clearly documented and tracked to resolution.

 - Able to articulate what changed, why, and confidence level in results to stakeholders without engineering escalation.



Interested candidates who thrive in structured, detail-heavy, and execution-focused roles are encouraged to apply and help us deliver trustworthy compliance intelligence at scale.

Applicants Privacy Policy https://k-id.com/job-applicants-privacy-notice

notes: k-ID is a tech startup with a strong focus on engineering and product development, with a few non-tech roles.
